parameters.agentDescription: The agent is designed to provide interactive cycle coaching to help amature cyclists train and compete at their best using a range of tools.

parameters.toolDescription: Get the athlete's personal profile including FTP, weight, goals, and availability. Use this to personalize advice based on their specific capabilities and objectives
parameters.query:
SELECT 
  json_build_object(
    'athlete_id', id,
    'name', name,
    'ftp', COALESCE(athlete_ftp, strava_ftp, 0),
    'weight_kg', COALESCE(strava_weight_kg, 0),
    'wkg', 
      CASE 
        WHEN COALESCE(strava_weight_kg, 0) > 0 
          THEN ROUND(COALESCE(athlete_ftp, strava_ftp, 0)::numeric / strava_weight_kg, 2)
        ELSE 0
      END,
    'training_goals', COALESCE(training_goal, 'Not specified'),
    'availability_hours', COALESCE(weekly_hours_available, 0),
    'target_event_date', target_event_date,
    'target_event_type', target_event_type,
    'weeks_to_event', weeks_to_event,
    'training_phase', training_phase,
    'profile_date', CURRENT_DATE,
    'strava_profile_synced', strava_profile_synced_at IS NOT NULL,
    'power_zones', strava_power_zones,
    'heart_rate_zones', strava_heart_rate_zones
  ) as athlete_data
FROM athletes 
WHERE id = {{ $json.metadata.athlete_id }};

WITH ride_details AS (
  SELECT 
    id,
    ride_date,
    COALESCE(title, 'Ride') as title,
    duration_min,
    tss,
    avg_power_watts,
    avg_heart_rate,
    time_in_power_zones,
    time_in_heart_rate_zones
  FROM rides 
  WHERE athlete_id = {{ $json.metadata.athlete_id }}
    AND ride_date >= CURRENT_DATE - INTERVAL '30 days'
  ORDER BY ride_date DESC
  LIMIT 10
),
ride_summary AS (
  SELECT 
    COUNT(*) as total_rides,
    COALESCE(SUM(tss), 0) as total_tss,
    ROUND(COALESCE(AVG(tss), 0), 1) as avg_tss,
    ROUND(COALESCE(AVG(avg_power_watts), 0), 0) as avg_power,
    COUNT(DISTINCT DATE(ride_date)) as days_with_rides
  FROM rides 
  WHERE athlete_id = {{ $json.metadata.athlete_id }}
    AND ride_date >= CURRENT_DATE - INTERVAL '30 days'
)
SELECT 
  json_build_object(
    'recent_rides', COALESCE(json_agg(
      json_build_object(
        'date', ride_date,
        'title', title,
        'duration_minutes', duration_min,
        'tss', tss,
        'avg_power', avg_power_watts,
        'avg_heart_rate', avg_heart_rate,
        'ftp_percentage', 
          CASE 
            WHEN avg_power_watts IS NOT NULL 
              THEN ROUND((avg_power_watts::numeric / NULLIF((SELECT COALESCE(athlete_ftp, strava_ftp, 242) FROM athletes WHERE id = {{ $json.metadata.athlete_id }}), 0)) * 100, 1)
            ELSE NULL
          END,
        'time_in_power_zones', time_in_power_zones,
        'time_in_heart_rate_zones', time_in_heart_rate_zones
      ) ORDER BY ride_date DESC
    ), '[]'),
    'summary', json_build_object(
      'total_rides', COALESCE((SELECT total_rides FROM ride_summary), 0),
      'total_tss', COALESCE((SELECT total_tss FROM ride_summary), 0),
      'avg_tss_per_ride', COALESCE((SELECT avg_tss FROM ride_summary), 0),
      'avg_power', COALESCE((SELECT avg_power FROM ride_summary), 0),
      'analysis_date', CURRENT_DATE,
      'days_with_rides', COALESCE((SELECT days_with_rides FROM ride_summary), 0)
    )
  ) as ride_data
FROM ride_details;

parameters.toolDescription: Get the athlete's current training phase, timeline, and progress toward goals. Use this to provide phase-appropriate advice and understand where they are in their training cycle.
parameters.query:
SELECT 
  json_build_object(
    'phase_name', phase_name,
    'focus', focus,
    'duration_weeks', duration_weeks,
    'start_date', start_date,
    'today_date', CURRENT_DATE,
    'days_until_start', GREATEST(0, start_date - CURRENT_DATE),
    'has_started', CURRENT_DATE >= start_date,
    'current_week', 
      CASE 
        WHEN CURRENT_DATE >= start_date THEN FLOOR((CURRENT_DATE - start_date) / 7) + 1
        ELSE 0
      END,
    'weeks_until_start', 
      CASE 
        WHEN CURRENT_DATE < start_date THEN CEIL((start_date - CURRENT_DATE) / 7.0)
        ELSE 0
      END,
    'rationale', rationale,
    'target_metrics', target_metrics,
    'is_active_phase', CURRENT_DATE >= start_date AND CURRENT_DATE < start_date + (duration_weeks * INTERVAL '7 days'),
    'phase_status',
      CASE 
        WHEN CURRENT_DATE < start_date THEN 'future'
        WHEN CURRENT_DATE >= start_date AND CURRENT_DATE < start_date + (duration_weeks * INTERVAL '7 days') THEN 'active'
        ELSE 'completed'
      END
  ) as phase_data
FROM training_phases 
WHERE athlete_id = {{ $json.metadata.athlete_id }}
  AND (completed IS NULL OR completed = false)
ORDER BY start_date DESC
LIMIT 1;

parameters.toolDescription: Search the training knowledge base for evidence-based principles, research, and best practices. Use this to provide scientifically-grounded advice and explain training concepts.

options.systemMessage:
=You are Dave, an experienced cycling coach having a real conversation with ${athleteName}.

=== DYNAMIC DATE SYSTEM ===
TODAY IS: ${currentDate} - This is ABSOLUTE TRUTH
CURRENT DATE (ISO): {{$json.metadata.today_iso}}
TIME: {{$json.metadata.current_time}} {{$json.metadata.timezone}}

=== TEMPORAL MATH ===
Ride data includes full ISO date timestamps (e.g. "2026-04-25T08:35:01+00:00").
Compare ride dates against TODAY'S DATE before describing them.
- If ride date matches today_iso: "today" or "this morning"
- If ride date = today_iso - 1: "yesterday"
- If ride date = today_iso - 2: "two days ago"
- NEVER assume a ride was "today" without checking the date against today_iso

=== LISTEN FIRST ===
Before using any tool, read this conversation first.
The last 25 messages contain everything the athlete has said.
Use tools only when you genuinely need data, not to re-read the conversation.
A good coach listens and remembers - don't make them repeat themselves.

=== FOLLOW-UP TRACKING ===
When the athlete says they WILL do something (a workout, check data, try a suggestion),
remember it. In future conversations, check if they did it and ask about it naturally.

=== CRITICAL RULES ===
1. USE TODAY'S DATE: ${currentDate} for all calculations
2. DO NOT hardcode specific dates (no "April 12th")
3. Event dates come FROM TOOLS, not assumptions
4. Calculate: event_date - today_date (using tool dates)
5. Trust tool math (days_until_start, phase_status, etc.)

=== CONVERSATION MEMORY ===
The current conversation (last 25 messages) is always available.
Read it before using any memory tools.
Only use search tools if the information is NOT in this conversation.

=== TOOL USAGE ===
Use tools COMBINATORIALLY:
• Conversation history + athlete data + training load + research
• For nutrition: Use research tools for evidence-based advice
• For dates: Use phase/goal tools, never assume

=== COACHING PHILOSOPHY: CRITICAL FRIEND ===
You are a CRITICAL FRIEND - supportive but will challenge when needed:
• COLLABORATIVE, NOT DIRECTIVE: We work together
• EVIDENCE-INFORMED, NOT DOGMATIC: Use research, not just opinions  
• ADAPTIVE, NOT RIGID: Adjust based on their feedback
• CHALLENGE ASSUMPTIONS: Question their thinking when needed
• HOLISTIC: Consider sleep, nutrition, life stress

=== CONVERSATION FLOW: NATURAL & CONSULTATIVE ===
• Be COLLABORATIVE, not directive - we work together
• Be CONSULTATIVE, not interrogative - understand their perspective
• Use questions STRATEGICALLY, not automatically
• Focus on immediate context unless asked for plans

=== STRATEGIC QUESTIONING - ABSOLUTE RULES ===
• YOU MUST NOT end every response with a question
• ENDING WITHOUT A QUESTION IS OFTEN BETTER
• When in doubt, END WITH A STATEMENT
• Only ask a question when you GENUINELY NEED INFORMATION to proceed

• Questions should FEEL NATURAL, not FORMULAIC
• Use questions to:
  1. Understand the athlete's perspective (consultative)
  2. Explore options together (collaborative)
  3. Check if advice resonates (adaptive)
  4. Move conversation forward purposefully

• DO NOT end every response with a question
• Sometimes the most consultative response is:
  - A thoughtful observation
  - A summary of key points  
  - An invitation to reflect
  - A statement that gives space

• When you DO ask a question:
  - Make it SINGLE and FOCUSED
  - Make it OPEN-ENDED (not yes/no)
  - Make it RELEVANT to their immediate concern
  - AVOID repetitive patterns ("What's your gut feeling...")

=== EMOTIONAL MOMENTS ===
• When athlete shares strong emotions (upset, demotivated, questioning):
  1. ACKNOWLEDGE the emotion fully first
  2. GIVE SPACE - don't immediately problem-solve
  3. It's OK to RESPOND without a question
  4. Sometimes SILENCE (in text) is powerful

=== AVOID THESE PATTERNS ===
• "Looking at your recent training..." (just discuss the training)
• "I can see..." boilerplate (integrate information naturally)
• Repeating athlete stats they already know
• Over-explaining your process - just coach
• Robotic data dumps - synthesize tool data into conversation
• Re-stating obvious context every response

=== RESPONSE STRUCTURE ===
1. ACKNOWLEDGE their immediate concern/goal
2. PROVIDE relevant advice using tools/data
3. CHALLENGE assumptions if needed (critical friend)
4. OPTIONALLY: Ask ONE focused question IF NEEDED to continue

=== ABSOLUTE: NO QUESTION WHEN EMOTIONAL ===
• When athlete shares emotions (upset, demotivated, questioning):
  DO NOT ask a question in your response
  ACKNOWLEDGE the emotion → GIVE SPACE → RESPOND without question
• Example BAD: "I'm sorry you feel that way. What specifically is bothering you?" ❌
• Example GOOD: "I'm sorry you feel that way. That kind of disappointment takes time to process." ✅

=== EXAMPLE: BAD vs GOOD ===
BAD: "I can see you have a race tomorrow. Looking at your data..."
GOOD: "Given your race is tomorrow, [direct response to concern]"

BAD: "You're at 242W FTP, 65kg..." 
GOOD: [Only mention stats if RELEVANT to the point]

=== WHEN TO CHALLENGE (CRITICAL FRIEND) ===
• If they're overtraining/undertraining
• If goals seem unrealistic vs current fitness
• If they're avoiding necessary hard work
• If nutrition/sleep habits need improvement
• Use research tools to support challenges

parameters.query:
SELECT 
  -- Extract clean content from ANY message format
  CASE 
    -- For AI messages with tool outputs: extract text AFTER the tool output
    WHEN message->>'type' = 'ai' AND message->>'content' LIKE '[Used tools:%] %' THEN
      TRIM(SUBSTRING(
        SPLIT_PART(message->>'content', '] ', 2)  -- Get text after closing bracket
        FROM 1 FOR 600
      ))
    
    -- For AI messages: clean up any remaining brackets/markup
    WHEN message->>'type' = 'ai' THEN
      TRIM(REGEXP_REPLACE(
        SUBSTRING(message->>'content' FROM 1 FOR 600),
        '\[.*?\]',  -- Remove any [bracketed] content
        ''
      ))
    
    -- For human messages: just take the content
    ELSE
      TRIM(SUBSTRING(message->>'content' FROM 1 FOR 600))
  END as content,
  
  -- Role mapping
  CASE 
    WHEN message->>'type' = 'human' THEN 'user'
    WHEN message->>'type' = 'ai' THEN 'assistant' 
    ELSE 'unknown'
  END as role,
  
  -- Timestamp (using ID order as proxy)
  NOW() - (ROW_NUMBER() OVER (ORDER BY id DESC) * INTERVAL '1 minute') as created_at
  
FROM n8n_chat_histories 
WHERE session_id IN ('{{$json.metadata.athlete_id}}', '={{$json.metadata.athlete_id}}')
ORDER BY id DESC 
LIMIT 15;

SELECT 
    target_event_type,
    target_event_date,
    target_event_distance_km,
    target_ftp_watts,
    target_weight_kg,
    weekly_hours_available,
    training_goal
FROM athletes 
WHERE id = {{$json.metadata.athlete_id}};

parameters.toolDescription: Search past conversations with the athlete for relevant information. Use this when you need to remember something the athlete said previously.

json.chatInput: can you remember what i said about my commute? how long it is?
